summary: D-121 static verification complete. The new stale-exempt-entry check in /app/frontend/lib/__tests__/i18n-sync-lint.test.ts (lines 369-397) is implemented correctly per spec: iterates Object.entries(KEYS_EXEMPT_FROM_UNUSED), parses file path via FILE_PATH_RX (/\b([a-zA-Z0-9_/\-]+\.tsx?)\b/), checks existsSync(join(REPO_ROOT, fpath)), parses backtick pattern via BACKTICK_PATTERN_RX (/`([^`]+)`/), splits pattern by /\$\{[^}]+\}/ filtering empty, and uses content.includes(part) on the source file. Missing parts are JSON.stringify-joined into the staleExempts message. Failure block (lines 436-445) is correctly placed AFTER deadExempts (lines 425-434) with the required prefix '[i18n-sync-lint] FEIL — N oppføringer i KEYS_EXEMPT_FROM_UNUSED har stale kilde-referanse (D-121)' and the fix-hint mentions both options (a) remove exempt + locale-keys, (b) update path/pattern. Success-line (line 463) ends with '(alle med valid kilde-referanse, D-121)'. yarn lint:all green in 2.64s (1483 keys × 4 langs, 1288 t() calls, 56 dynamic exempts, all valid kilde-referanse). yarn tsc --noEmit PASS in 2.22s. yarn build PASS in 24.42s. Robustness test executed (twice): (1) corrupted file path 'TenantViewer.tsx' → 'NonExistent.tsx' for admin_tenants.wizard_step1 → lint correctly failed with exit 1 and exact message '❌ admin_tenants.wizard_step1 — kildefil components/platform/NonExistent.tsx finnes ikke'. (2) corrupted backtick pattern 'pwd_score.${score}' → 'pwd_score_BOGUS.${score}' → lint correctly failed with exit 1 and exact message '❌ pwd_score.0 — mønster pwd_score_BOGUS.${score} mangler 1 bit i lib/password-strength.ts: "pwd_score_BOGUS."'. Both edits reverted; final yarn lint:i18n-sync green again. No files left in modified state.

critical_code_review_comments: Implementation matches spec exactly. The regex FILE_PATH_RX is intentionally simple — it matches the first .ts/.tsx path-like token in the comment, which works because all 56 current exempt comments start with the path before the em-dash. If anyone ever writes a comment like 'see also other.tsx — t(`x.${n}`) in foo.tsx', only the first match is checked. Not a problem today, but worth a one-line comment noting this assumption.

Pattern check correctly validates static parts via content.includes() — this is a substring match, not a token match. So if you had pattern `foo.${n}` and the file had unrelated text containing 'foo.', the check would falsely pass. Acceptable trade-off (false-negative is the conservative direction for a sanity-lint), but worth noting in case stricter checking is desired later.

Minor: the comment in the helper KEYS_EXEMPT_FROM_UNUSED docblock (line 58-59) says '53 dynamic-eksempt' but Object.keys length is now 56 — number drifted with later additions. Cosmetic, not functional. Could be updated to 'NN' or removed.
Minor: the fix-hint message uses '(a) ... (b) ...' — matches spec. Both options are clearly stated.
